render_data.title: Rate of biodegradation of three synthetic-rubber samples
render_data.prompt: Sample C biodegrades fastest and plateaus highest; B is slowest. Choose a sample, press Play to build its curve, then drag the cursor to read the biodegradation % at any time.
render_data.task: List the three samples in order of increasing particle size and justify your answer.
render_data.answer: Increasing particle size: C < A < B. C has the smallest particles (largest surface area), so it biodegrades fastest; B has the largest particles, so it is slowest.
render_data.trend: Smaller particle size means greater surface area, so a faster rate of biodegradation.

render_data.title: Average time taken for the firefly light to stop vs temperature
render_data.prompt: Press Play to trace the curve, then drag the cursor to read the average light duration at any temperature.
render_data.task: Predict the time taken for the firefly light to stop at 10 °C.
render_data.answer: At 10 °C the light lasts about 440 minutes (read off the curve). At 20 °C it lasts 240 minutes.
render_data.trend: As temperature increases, the bioluminescent reaction is faster, so the light stops sooner (duration decreases).
